body:
## Summary
- Create a linked follow-up issue for concrete `/orchestrate` patch requests on closed or merged PRs
- Dispatch implementation against the new issue and comment back on the source PR
- Stop ambiguous closed-PR orchestration with a visible explanation
- Forward source comment URLs into the orchestrator and document the behavior

## Verification
- `npm --prefix .agent run build`
- `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js`
- `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.js`

Closes #43

comments.body:
Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `implement` -> `review` (round 3/10).

agent planner selected review: Implementation succeeded and produced PR #45 for issue #43; no review has run yet, so the next useful automated step is review.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777507048847 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUxMzk4NjEwMjY6aW1wbGVtZW50OjQzOnJldmlldzo0NToz -->

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #45 addresses #43 by allowing `/orchestrate` on merged or closed PRs to turn concrete follow-up patch requests into fresh issue-backed implementation work.
- It adds closed-PR request classification, follow-up issue creation, source PR comments, implement dispatch, source comment URL forwarding, docs, and regression coverage.
- The intended behavior matches the issue’s acceptance criteria: concrete code follow-ups should continue against `main`, while ambiguous closed-PR requests should stop visibly.
- The main remaining risk is operational idempotency during reruns, cancellations, or failed dispatches.

## Review
- The implementation is directionally correct and keeps delegated `implement` authorization in front of the new side effect.
- However, the follow-up issue is created before the existing handoff dedupe path can use a stable target, so normal workflow reruns can duplicate issues and dispatches.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Follow-up issue creation is not idempotent | BLOCKING | The new closed-PR path creates an issue before the handoff marker/dedupe block runs, and the dedupe key later uses the newly-created issue number. Rerunning the same orchestration can create another issue and dispatch again instead of finding the prior handoff. |

## Progress
- The PR already adds docs for closed/merged PR follow-up orchestration.
- It adds happy-path coverage for creating a follow-up issue and dispatching `implement`.
- It adds coverage that delegated `implement` authorization is checked before issue creation.
- I posted one inline comment on `.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts` line 310 for the blocking idempotency issue.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Follow-up issue creation is not idempotent</summary>

**Cause:** `createFollowupIssueForClosedPr(targetNumber)` runs inside `decideManualOrchestration()` before the existing handoff dedupe block. Because the returned decision points at the newly-created issue, the dedupe key and marker lookup are based on a fresh issue number each time.

**Candidate solutions:** Make the closed-PR follow-up destination stable before creating a new issue. For example, find/reuse an existing source PR/comment marker, create a deterministic marker on the source PR before side effects, or otherwise record enough state on the source target to recover the prior follow-up issue before creating another one. Add a regression test that rerunning the same closed-PR follow-up does not create a second issue or dispatch.

**Comments:** This matters for normal GitHub Actions behavior: reruns, cancellations, and failures after issue creation but before/around dispatch should not duplicate user-visible work.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: the unresolved issue requires a concrete branch change and is safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
NEEDS_REWORK

## Action Items
- [ ] Make closed-PR follow-up issue creation idempotent before side effects and add rerun regression coverage.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 4/10).

agent planner selected fix-pr: Latest review synthesis verdict is NEEDS_REWORK with Recommended Next Step FIX_PR; the unresolved issue is a concrete blocking idempotency bug safe for automated repair.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777507331633 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUxNDAwNjc0NzI6cmV2aWV3OjQ1OmZpeF9wcjo0NTo0 -->

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/implement-issue-43/codex-25139861026`. Requested by @lolipopshock.

- Made closed/merged PR follow-up issue creation idempotent by recording a stable source-PR follow-up marker before creating the issue.
- Reruns now reuse the recorded follow-up issue and let the existing handoff marker dedupe prevent duplicate dispatches.
- Added regression coverage for rerunning the same closed-PR follow-up without creating a second issue or dispatch.
- Verification: `npm --prefix .agent run build`; `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js`; `git diff --check`.
@sepo-agent /answer why did the orchestration stopp in the middle

It stopped after the `fix-pr` pass because the follow-up orchestrator planner failed to produce a valid planner decision.

What happened:

- The chain did continue through `implement -> review -> fix-pr`.
- The `fix-pr` workflow succeeded and dispatched the next `agent-orchestrator.yml` run.
- That orchestrator run was in `agent` mode, so it needed the planner to return valid JSON for the next handoff.
- The planner tried to run `gh pr view 45 ...` to inspect the latest PR state, hit an execution-permission prompt, and that prompt was aborted.
- Because the planner response was missing/invalid, the handoff code stopped with: `Handoff stop: agent planner decision missing or invalid`.

Relevant run: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/actions/runs/25140345554

Relevant code paths:
- `fix-pr` does hand back to orchestration when `orchestration_enabled == true`: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/agent/implement-issue-43/codex-25139861026/.github/workflows/agent-fix-pr.yml#L277-L297
- In `agent` mode, a missing planner decision becomes a stop: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/agent/implement-issue-43/codex-25139861026/.agent/src/handoff.ts#L296-L301
- The docs describe that `agent` mode depends on planner JSON for action-originated handoffs: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/agent/implement-issue-43/codex-25139861026/.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md#L72-L76

So this was not the max-round limit. It was an orchestration planner failure caused by the planner requesting a `gh` read under a permission mode that could not approve it, which then yielded no valid handoff decision.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `orchestrate` -> `review` (round 2/10).

manual orchestrate start on PR; dispatching review

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777520028490 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUxNDU4OTc1OTk6b3JjaGVzdHJhdGU6NDU6cmV2aWV3OjQ1OjI -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 90 | PARTIAL | 7 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | Closed-PR follow-up revalidates downstream `implement` access before issue creation/dispatch: [`orchestrate-handoff.ts#L406-L407`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/fbf98e0872293d6d45937edbcb9ee8593f4d39b0/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L406-L407), covered by [`orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.ts#L288-L306`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/fbf98e0872293d6d45937edbcb9ee8593f4d39b0/.agent/src/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.ts#L288-L306). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Reuses existing access-policy helpers, `createIssue`, `dispatchWorkflow`, and handoff marker utilities instead of rebuilding route policy or dispatch logic. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Orchestrator docs now describe source comment URLs and closed/merged PR follow-up behavior: [`agent-orchestrator.md#L40-L74`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/fbf98e0872293d6d45937edbcb9ee8593f4d39b0/.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md#L40-L74). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Comment on accepted workflow stops | partial | 4/6 | Ambiguous closed-PR requests get a visible source-PR stop comment: [`orchestrate-handoff.ts#L396-L403`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/fbf98e0872293d6d45937edbcb9ee8593f4d39b0/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L396-L403). Policy-denied concrete follow-ups return `authStop` without a visible source comment: [`orchestrate-handoff.ts#L406-L407`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/fbf98e0872293d6d45937edbcb9ee8593f4d39b0/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L406-L407). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | partial | 5/7 | Dispatch failures mark handoff state failed and comment back: [`orchestrate-handoff.ts#L664-L687`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/fbf98e0872293d6d45937edbcb9ee8593f4d39b0/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L664-L687). However, a failure after issue creation but before updating the source marker with the issue number can still lose retry continuity: [`orchestrate-handoff.ts#L456-L484`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/fbf98e0872293d6d45937edbcb9ee8593f4d39b0/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L456-L484). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Diff is limited to orchestrator runtime, workflows forwarding the new input, docs, and focused tests. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 6/7 | The implementation is direct and deterministic; the added marker/idempotency flow is more involved, but justified by rerun and duplicate-dispatch behavior. |

## Notes

- Strong positives: downstream authorization is checked before side effects, docs were updated with behavior changes, and regression tests cover issue creation, idempotent reruns, ambiguous-stop comments, and authorization denial.
- Main rubric gaps are around visibility for policy stops and one narrow state-continuity failure window after creating a follow-up issue.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** `comment-on-accepted-workflow-stops` is only partially satisfied. A concrete closed-PR follow-up denied by delegated `implement` authorization stops without commenting on the source PR, even though the workflow accepted an explicit `/orchestrate` request.
- **WARNING:** `surface-stateful-failures` is mostly satisfied, but if `createIssue` succeeds and the reservation marker update at [`orchestrate-handoff.ts#L477-L484`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/fbf98e0872293d6d45937edbcb9ee8593f4d39b0/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L477-L484) fails, the created issue number is not durably captured. A later retry can treat the targetless reservation as failed and create another issue.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PARTIAL

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #45 addresses #43 by allowing concrete `/orchestrate` follow-up requests on closed or merged PRs to continue as fresh issue-backed implementation work.
- It creates a linked follow-up issue, forwards the source comment URL, dispatches `implement`, and comments back on the source PR.
- It also stops ambiguous closed-PR orchestration requests with a visible explanation and documents the new behavior.
- A prior blocking duplicate-issue concern was fixed by adding a stable closed-PR follow-up marker before issue creation.

## Review
- The PR is close, and the main idempotency problem from the earlier review appears fixed for normal reruns.
- Two warning-level gaps remain: one narrow state-continuity failure window after issue creation, and one accepted-stop path that is not visible on the source PR.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Follow-up issue can be orphaned if marker update fails | WARNING | If issue creation succeeds but the source PR reservation marker update fails, a retry can lose the created issue number and later create a duplicate follow-up issue. |
| Policy-denied closed-PR follow-ups stop silently | WARNING | Delegated `implement` authorization failures return a stop decision before creating an issue, but do not comment on the source PR even though the explicit `/orchestrate` request was accepted. |

## Progress
- The earlier blocking duplicate-dispatch issue is addressed for normal reruns by reserving and reusing a stable closed-PR follow-up marker.
- Delegated `implement` authorization is checked before creating the follow-up issue.
- Docs and focused tests were added for closed/merged PR follow-up orchestration.
- Existing PR discussion already notes both remaining warning-level concerns, so I did not add duplicate inline comments.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Follow-up issue can be orphaned if marker update fails</summary>

**Cause:** After `createFollowupIssueForClosedPr()` succeeds, the issue number is only recoverable once the reservation marker is updated with the target issue. If that update fails, the PR is left with a target-less pending marker; after TTL expiry, a retry can mark it failed and create another issue.

**Candidate solutions:** Persist or recover the target issue in this failure window. Options include posting a target-bearing fallback marker/comment, searching for an existing follow-up issue that links the same source PR/comment before creating a new one, or adding a retry path around the marker update. Add regression coverage for the post-create marker-update failure.

**Comments:** Both reviewers flagged this as the main remaining runtime risk.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Policy-denied closed-PR follow-ups stop silently</summary>

**Cause:** The closed-PR concrete follow-up path returns `authStop` immediately when delegated `implement` authorization fails, without commenting back to the source PR.

**Candidate solutions:** When this accepted `/orchestrate` request is denied by downstream route policy, post a concise source-PR comment explaining that `implement` access is required and no follow-up issue was created. Add focused test coverage for the visible stop.

**Comments:** This is a rubric compliance gap rather than a correctness failure in dispatch authorization.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: the remaining findings are concrete branch changes and are safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Make the follow-up issue recoverable if the post-create reservation marker update fails, with regression coverage.
- [ ] Comment on the source PR when a concrete closed-PR follow-up is denied by delegated `implement` authorization.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 3/10).

agent planner selected fix-pr: Source review concluded MINOR_ISSUES within round budget, so one targeted fix-pr pass is warranted for the remaining concrete branch-level review feedback.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777520784270 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUxNDYxMjkzODg6Zml4X3ByOjQ1OnJldmlldzo0NTo0 -->

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #45 addresses #43 by allowing concrete `/orchestrate` follow-up requests on merged or closed PRs to continue as fresh issue-backed implementation work.
- It creates a linked follow-up issue, forwards the source comment URL, dispatches `implement`, and comments back on the source PR.
- It also stops ambiguous closed-PR requests visibly and documents the new behavior.
- Prior review findings around duplicate follow-up issue creation, marker persistence, and policy-denial visibility have been addressed by follow-up fixes.

## Review
- The latest implementation is close and the prior state-continuity issues appear resolved.
- One warning remains: the closed-PR request classifier is broad enough to turn diagnostic or ambiguous non-code requests into implementation work.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Broad keyword matching can dispatch non-code questions | WARNING | The classifier treats words like `failing`, `failure`, `test`, or `workflow` as enough to create a follow-up issue, which can misclassify diagnostic questions that should stop visibly. |

## Progress
- The earlier blocking duplicate-issue/idempotency concern was fixed with stable closed-PR follow-up markers.
- The post-create marker update failure path now writes a fallback target-bearing marker.
- Policy-denied concrete closed-PR follow-ups now comment on the source PR.
- Focused verification reported by the reviewer passed: build plus `orchestrate-handoff-cli` and `envelope` tests.
- I posted one new inline comment for the remaining classifier issue on `.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts` line 251.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Broad keyword matching can dispatch non-code questions</summary>

**Cause:** `isLikelyBranchChangeRequest()` currently accepts a single broad keyword, including `failing`, `failure`, `test`, or `workflow`, after stripping the `/orchestrate` command. That can classify requests like “why is the workflow failing?” or “can you test this?” as concrete patch work.

**Candidate solutions:** Tighten the predicate to require clearer change intent, add diagnostic/question exclusions, or combine verb intent with code-change terms. Add regression coverage for an ambiguous “workflow failing” style closed-PR request that should stop visibly instead of creating a follow-up issue.

**Comments:** This is directly tied to #43’s acceptance criterion that ambiguous non-code requests still stop safely with a visible explanation.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: the remaining finding is a concrete branch change with straightforward regression coverage and is safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Tighten closed-PR follow-up classification so ambiguous diagnostic requests do not dispatch `implement`, with regression coverage.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 5/10).

agent planner selected fix-pr: Latest review result is MINOR_ISSUES, round budget remains, and PR #45 still has concrete branch-level follow-up work after the prior fix pass.

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |

| 100 | PASS | 9 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | Delegated `implement` authorization is centralized and checked before closed-PR issue creation/dispatch, with a visible stop on denial: [`orchestrate-handoff.ts#L209-L231`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/2b058d4f2750a49381cb6c10c5b2a1ab847b7df7/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L209-L231), [`#L438-L445`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/2b058d4f2750a49381cb6c10c5b2a1ab847b7df7/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L438-L445), covered by [`orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.ts#L331-L350`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/2b058d4f2750a49381cb6c10c5b2a1ab847b7df7/.agent/src/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.ts#L331-L350). |

| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Orchestrator docs now describe source comment URLs and closed/merged PR follow-up behavior: [`agent-orchestrator.md#L40-L47`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/2b058d4f2750a49381cb6c10c5b2a1ab847b7df7/.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md#L40-L47), [`#L65-L74`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/2b058d4f2750a49381cb6c10c5b2a1ab847b7df7/.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md#L65-L74). |

| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | Closed-PR follow-up reservations are durable, stale reservations are failed before retry, issue-creation failures update markers, and post-create marker update failures write a fallback target-bearing marker: [`orchestrate-handoff.ts#L376-L402`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/2b058d4f2750a49381cb6c10c5b2a1ab847b7df7/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L376-L402), [`#L448-L520`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/2b058d4f2750a49381cb6c10c5b2a1ab847b7df7/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L448-L520), covered by [`orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.ts#L259-L329`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/2b058d4f2750a49381cb6c10c5b2a1ab847b7df7/.agent/src/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.ts#L259-L329). |

## Notes

- The latest PR head addresses the earlier rubric-specific gaps: policy-denied closed-PR follow-ups now comment visibly, post-create marker persistence has fallback coverage, and diagnostic closed-PR requests stop instead of dispatching implementation.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific findings remain.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #45 addresses #43 by allowing concrete `/orchestrate` follow-up requests on merged or closed PRs to continue as fresh issue-backed implementation work.
- It creates a linked follow-up issue, forwards the source comment URL, dispatches `implement`, and comments back on the source PR.
- It also stops ambiguous closed-PR requests visibly, checks delegated `implement` authorization before side effects, and documents the new behavior.
- The latest reviewer inputs agree that prior concerns around idempotency, marker persistence, policy-denial visibility, and broad diagnostic classification are resolved.
